subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Biosignatures: Signs of Life
description: A quick, practical guide to Biosignatures: Signs of Life.

hook.text: Biosignatures are atmospheric gases that could indicate life. Oxygen, methane, and ozone are key targets.

buildup.text: On Earth, oxygen is overwhelmingly produced by photosynthesis. Without life, it would vanish from our atmosphere.

discovery.text: Finding oxygen and methane together would be compelling—they destroy each other without a living source to replenish them.

twist.text: Abiotic processes can also produce oxygen and methane. A single gas alone is never proof—context matters enormously.

climax.text: JWST and future missions aim to detect these gases. A credible biosignature detection would transform human understanding.

punchline.text: Oxygen plus methane together. That combination shouldn't exist without life.

quiz.question: Why would finding oxygen and methane together in an atmosphere be significant?
options: They react and destroy each other, so both need a continuous source like life to coexist
Together they form water, which is essential for life
They are only found in atmospheres of planets with magnetic fields
